name: Jazz Essentials
description: Core jazz chord progressions every guitarist should know

progressions.name: ii-V-I Major
progressions.description: The most common jazz progression. Foundation of jazz harmony.

progressions.name: ii-V-I Minor
progressions.description: Minor key ii-V-I with half-diminished ii chord.

progressions.name: I-vi-ii-V (Rhythm Changes A)
progressions.description: Rhythm changes A section turnaround. 'I Got Rhythm', 'Oleo', 'Anthropology'.

progressions.name: Rhythm Changes Bridge
progressions.description: Rhythm changes B section: cycle of dominants.

progressions.name: Jazz Blues (Bird Changes)
progressions.description: 12-bar blues with Charlie Parker substitutions. 'Blues for Alice', 'Au Privave'.

progressions.name: Autumn Leaves (A Section)
progressions.description: Joseph Kosma. The most-called jazz standard.

progressions.name: All The Things You Are (A Section)
progressions.description: Jerome Kern. Moves through multiple key centers.

progressions.name: So What / Modal Vamp
progressions.description: Miles Davis. Dorian mode with half-step shift. Quartal voicings shine here.

progressions.name: ii-V-I with Tritone Sub
progressions.description: Replace V7 with bII7 (same tritone, different root). Smooth chromatic bass line.

progressions.name: Coltrane Changes (Giant Steps)
progressions.description: John Coltrane. Three key centers a major third apart.

progressions.name: Backdoor ii-V
progressions.description: bVIIm7 to bVII7 resolving to I. Common in pop-jazz and film scores.

progressions.name: Lady Bird Turnaround
progressions.description: Tadd Dameron's turnaround: I to bIIImaj7 to bVImaj7 to bIImaj7.

progressions.name: Stella by Starlight (Bridge)
progressions.description: Victor Young. Dense harmonic movement through multiple keys.

progressions.name: Girl From Ipanema (A Section)
progressions.description: Antonio Carlos Jobim. Bossa nova classic with Lydian flavor.
